recommendations: add an automated smoke-test CI job for `mcp_output/start_mcp.py` that validates server startup and tool registration
split MCP endpoints into “stable” (core runtime: `psychopy.plugins`
`psychopy.experiment.plugins`
`psychopy.hardware.manager`) vs “experimental” (GUI/plugin-manager/session wrappers) and expose only stable by default
add strict input/output schemas (Pydantic models) and argument validation for device/plugin/session operations to prevent unsafe calls
implement capability/permission gating for high-risk tools (`openDevice`
`installPlugin`
`uninstallPlugin`
`start/stop`) with explicit allowlists
add structured error mapping (PsychoPy exceptions → MCP error codes/messages) with actionable remediation text
introduce lazy imports and dependency guards for optional stacks (wx/Qt/pygame/eyetracker SDKs) so MCP import mode degrades gracefully
add contract tests for every exported endpoint (happy path + failure modes + optional dependency missing)
add integration tests for plugin lifecycle flows (discover→load→activate
install→scan→uninstall) in isolated temp environments
add concurrency/time-out controls around hardware and session calls to avoid hangs in long-running MCP processes
add observability (JSON logs
per-tool latency
error rate
dependency-missing counters) and document troubleshooting in `README_MCP.md`
pin and align MCP dependency versions with PsychoPy’s `pyproject.toml` constraints and add a lockfile/reproducible install path
replace broad endpoint exposure names like `session`/`devicemanager` with explicit verb-based tools and deprecate ambiguous aliases
add a minimal headless CLI fallback path for key operations (`psyexpCompile`
plugin listing) when import initialization fails
create a compatibility matrix (OS × Python × optional backends) and gate unsupported tools at runtime
run security hardening for plugin install/uninstall surfaces (path validation
source restrictions
no arbitrary shell execution)
add regression tests for zip-fallback/import environments since analysis indicates non-standard import paths
reduce risk by wrapping PsychoPy APIs behind a thin adapter interface with version checks and feature flags for future upstream changes

success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all expected nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-mode adapter strategy was appropriate for PsychoPy core APIs (plugins, experiment plugins, hardware manager, session).
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio, indicating baseline runtime viability.
Repository structure and pyproject metadata were sufficient to derive integration anchors despite missing requirements.txt/environment.yml.
failure_reasons: Quality gaps: original project test validation did not pass (or was not effectively executed), and generated metrics are incomplete (LOC/size/perf all zero), reducing confidence in production readiness.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external semantic enrichment for endpoint selection and risk triage.

node_performance.download_time: Not separately instrumented; repository ingest succeeded via zip fallback over a large codebase (~931 files).
node_performance.analysis_time: Likely dominant phase due to repository size/complexity; analysis completed successfully with medium complexity outputs.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; 7 MCP artifacts generated with import adapter and endpoint mapping.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed quickly; however, original project tests were not meaningfully validated in this run.

resource_usage.disk_usage: Low incremental MCP output footprint, but generated size metrics are missing (reported 0).
